entry.title: DEPUTY JAILS WOMAN FOR WARRANT ON WEDNESDAY
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 10:40 a.m. A deputy with the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office jailed a woman for a warrant on Wednesday night. A DCSO report said just after 9:30 p.m. the deputy contacted a woman in a parking lot after houses at a business in the 3400 block of Old Highway 99 South, south of...

entry.title: SEMI-TRUCK RAMS LARGE PICKUP IN RICE HILL AREA
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 10:30 a.m. A semi-truck and a large pickup collided in the Rice Hill area on Wednesday. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said at 3:30 p.m. the driver of the large pickup was traveling north on John Long Road. The semi-truck was exiting Interstate Five north and ended up ramming the first...

entry.title: PICKUP LEAVES ROADWAY AND OVERTURNS ON HIGHWAY 230
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 10:10 a.m. A pickup left the roadway and overturned on Highway 230 on Wednesday afternoon. An Oregon State Police report said the accident took place just before 3:30 p.m. near milepost 20 eastbound. The pickup exited the roadway, overturned, and came to rest thirty feet off the pavement. Due to the...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL THREE PEOPLE FOR PROHIBITED CAMPING
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 9:50 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed three people for prohibited camping on Wednesday. An RPD report said at 11:30 a.m. four people ranging in age from 41 to 57 were all camping in a tent just south of the Deer Creek Bridge in the 200 block of Southeast Pine Street. This was a...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR REPORTED STORE THEFT
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 9:40 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a reported store theft on Wednesday. An RPD report said shortly before 2:00 p.m. loss prevention staff detained a 22-year-old man for a theft from a store in the 2500 block of Northwest Stewart Parkway. The man admitted to leaving the store without paying...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL DILLARD MAN FOR ALLEGED TRESPASS INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 9:30 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a Dillard man following an alleged trespass incident on Wednesday. An RPD report said just before 12:00 p.m. officers responded to a disturbance in the 2100 block of Southeast Douglas Avenue. The 20-year-old suspect was reportedly intoxicated and had gone to the home in an attempt to...

entry.title: PEDESTRIAN TAKEN TO HOSPITAL AFTER BEING HIT BY VEHICLE.
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 9:15 a.m. A pedestrian was taken to the hospital after being hit by a vehicle on Wednesday evening. A Roseburg Police report said just after 7:30 p.m. the man was walking across the sidewalk in the 1600 block of Northwest Troost Street when a motorist drove her vehicle into the side of...

entry.title: Police Log: March 4, 2026
entry.summary: Roseburg police reported a theft arrest at Sherms, multiple prohibited-camping lodgings near Deer Creek, a trespass/harassment arrest after a disturbance, a vehicle vs. pedestrian crash near Roseburg Christian Academy, a public drinking citation, and a DUII arrest.

entry.title: MORNING CONVERSATION 03.05.06
entry.summary: Live from Salem, State Representative Alek Skarlatos talks about what is ahead for the final few days of the short session of the Oregon State Legislature.

entry.title: 5th Annual Music in the Courthouse brings together schools from across county
entry.summary: On a normal day, the Douglas County Courthouse is filled with lawyers, deputies and judges. But on Wednesday, the usual crowd was joined by a much more irregular one: Musicians.

entry.title: Read the Room bookstore and coffee shop celebrates grand opening in Sutherlin
entry.summary: SUTHERLIN — Many would consider retirement from their career as a time to enjoy a future of doing sweet nothing. But not Julie Otley. Instead of using it as a chance to kick back, she's pursuing a dream: owning her…

entry.title: Bergin College Seeks Volunteer Puppy Raisers for Four Dogs Needing Homes by Next Week to Help Change Lives
entry.summary: Bergin College of Canine Studies needs volunteer puppy raisers by next week for four dogs: Trendy (2), Zabin (9 months), Antje (5 months) and Amanda (5 months). The home stage builds the confidence and routines future service dogs need.

entry.title: March 5, 2026
entry.summary: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop

entry.title: WYDEN PRESSES ADMINISTRATION OVER WILDFIRE PREPAREDNESS
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 5:40 a.m. On Wednesday, U.S. Senator Ron Wyden called on the Trump administration to provide a detailed plan for how it plans to help communities in Oregon and the West prepare for what has the potential to be a catastrophic fire season amid record-low snowpack and worsening drought conditions. A Wyden release...

entry.title: STRAY SHOWERS THURSDAY, HIGH PRESSURE MOVING IN FOR THE WEEKEND
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 4:00 a.m. The Umpqua Valley is expecting some stray showers on Thursday with high pressure moving in later Friday and sticking around for the upcoming weekend. Forecasters at the National Weather Service in Medford told News Radio 93-9 FM and 1240 KQEN that not much rain is likely Thursday. Saturday and Sunday...

entry.title: DILLARD MDF CONSTRUCTION HAS RESTARTED
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 3:50 a.m. In the KQEN Business Spotlight: On Wednesday, Roseburg Forest Products announced it is restarting construction activities at Dillard MDF. An RFP release said it is a new medium density fiberboard plant at the heart of the company’s long-term growth strategy and its goal to become the leading producer of MDF...

entry.title: GRAND OPENING FOR TENNIS, PICKLEBALL COURTS ON THURSDAY
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 3:40 a.m. A ribbon-cutting ceremony and grand opening will be held for Roseburg’s new $2.5 million outdoor tennis and pickleball facility on Thursday. A city release said the public is invited to attend the ceremony and view the eight newly renovated tennis courts, ten dedicated pickleball courts and new fencing, lighting, seating,...

entry.title: ROSEBURG FIRE DEPARTMENT HYDRANT FLUSHING TO BEGIN APRIL 1ST
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 3:30 a.m. The Roseburg Fire Department will be flushing fire hydrants inside the city limits of Roseburg from April 1st, through June 30th. Hydrant flushing will be conducted Monday through Friday each week from 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. A city release said the fire department realizes that hydrant flushing may cause...

entry.title: BLM CLOSING PART OF RICE CREEK ROAD STARTING FRIDAY
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 3:20 a.m. The Bureau of Land Management is replacing a deteriorating culvert and will temporarily close a portion of Rice Creek Road, also known as BLM Road 29-7-24.0, starting on Friday. That road is located off Willis Creek Road, southwest of Winston. A BLM release said construction is expected to last two...

entry.title: APPLICATIONS OPEN FOR UEDP GROWTH TALKS PITCH NIGHT
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 3:05 a.m. Umpqua Economic Development Partnership is bringing back the Umpqua Growth Talks Pitch Night events for 2026. The Pitch Night program aims to provide financial and advisory support for emerging businesses in Douglas County, fostering their growth and encouraging success. Since its inception in 2019, Pitch Night events have generated nearly...

entry.title: UMPQUA SYMPHONY ASSOCIATION PRESENTS FANDANGO! MARCH 14TH
entry.summary: March 5, 2026 2:50 a.m. In the KQEN Cultural Spotlight: The Umpqua Symphony Association will present Fandango! in concert on Saturday, March 14th at 3:00 p.m. at Centerstage Theater on the Umpqua Community College campus. Fandango! takes its name from its signature piece, the last movement of the “Fandango Quintet by Boccherini. Touted as...

entry.title: Roseburg Forest Products Reboots Dillard MDF Project, Plans 140+ Jobs When Plant Opens
entry.summary: Roseburg Forest Products restarted construction March 4, 2026 on its Dillard MDF plant, featuring a Siempelkamp continuous press to make MDF and HDF from wood residuals. The company expects 140+ jobs when operations begin in late 2028.

entry.title: INSIDE DOUGLAS COUNTY 03.04.26
entry.summary: Live from Salem, State Representative Virgle Osborne talks about what is happening during the final days of the short session of the Oregon State Legislature.

entry.title: Construction restarts at RFP's new Dillard MDF plant
entry.summary: DILLARD — Almost six months after announcing the discontinuation of operations at its Dillard hardwood plywood facility and departure from the hardwood plywood market altogether, Roseburg Forest Products (RFP) announced Wednesday that it is restarting construction at Dillard MDF. This…

entry.title: GLENDALE MAN JAILED FOR ALLEGED ESCAPE/RESIST INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 10:50 a.m. A Glendale man was jailed by the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office following an alleged escape and resist incident on Tuesday. A DCSO report said at approximately 1:40 p.m. deputies went into a business in the 400 block of Pacific Avenue in Glendale to contact the 52-year-old who another deputy had...

entry.title: GLIDE MAN JAILED FOR WARRANT
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 10:15 a.m. Deputies with the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office jailed a man for a warrant on Tuesday afternoon. A DCSO report said at 2:30 p.m. the 40-year-old showed up at Saving Grace Humane Society on Old Del Rio Road in Winchester to pick up his dog which had been held due to...

entry.title: DEPUTIES JAIL TRANSIENT FOR WARRANTS, CITE FOR METHAMPHETAMINE
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 10:00 a.m. Deputies with the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office jailed a transient for warrants and cited him for a drug law violation on Tuesday afternoon. An RPD report said just after 12:40 p.m. deputies contacted the 36-year-old man in the 1400 block of Northeast Rocky Ridge Drive in Roseburg, due to trespassing...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE CITE MAN FOR WARRANT
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 9:50 a.m. Roseburg Police cited a man for a warrant on Tuesday morning. An RPD report said just after 7:50 a.m. the 37-year-old was contacted regarding a littering complain near the intersection of Northeast O’Dell Avenue and Northeast Caspar Street. He was found to have a warrant for his arrest out...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE CITE MAN FOR PROHIBITED CAMPING
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 9;40 a.m. Roseburg Police cited a man for prohibited camping early Wednesday morning. An RPD report said at 5:50 a.m. officers located the 38-year-old camping within ten feet of the sidewalk in the 400 feet of Northeast Stephens Street. This was outside of the allotted area. The man was issued a criminal...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR DUII FOLLOWING VEHICLE CRASH
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 9:30 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for DUII following a vehicle crash on Tuesday night. An RPD report said just after 9:50 p.m. officers observed a sedan quickly pulling off a side street of the 1400 block of Southeast Pine Street. The vehicle then swerved from the left shoulder to the...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR WARRANTS
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 9:20 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for warrants on Tuesday. An RPD report said at 4:45 p.m. officer contacted the 62-year-old in the 400 block of Southeast Jackson Street. They knew he had warrants from Douglas County Circuit Court and RPD. Dispatch confirmed the warrants and the man was taken into...

entry.title: MORNING CONVERSATION 03.04.26
entry.summary: Peace at Home Advocacy Center Executive Director Melanie Prummer makes the announcement about how much money they raised at their Empower event on Saturday night, and runs through the list of the top People’s Choice winners.

entry.title: Police Log: March 3, 2026
entry.summary: Roseburg police reported arrests for warrants (including meth found at jail), a DUII-related crash on SE Pine, and a criminal prohibited-camping citation near NE Garden Valley, according to the city’s daily police log.

entry.title: Earth Day and Energy Fair 2026 Planned in Downtown Roseburg
entry.summary: Earth Day and Energy Fair 2026 is set for Saturday, April 18, 11 a.m. to 4 p.m. on SE Main Street in downtown Roseburg. The free public event will feature exhibits, info tables, kids activities, and sessions on energy, gardening, native plants and more.

entry.title: March 4, 2026
entry.summary: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop

entry.title: WINTER WEATHER ADVISORY IN EFFECT THROUGH 10:00 P.M. WEDNESDAY
entry.summary: March 6, 2026 4:00 a.m. A Winter Weather Advisory remains in effect through 10:00 p.m. Wednesday for mountain areas in parts of southern Oregon. Forecasters at the National Weather Service in Medford said snow is expected above 4,000 feet. Winds are forecast to gust as high as 45 miles per hour over high and exposed...

entry.title: GOVERNOR SIGNS BILL MOVING GAS TAX REFERENDUM
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 3:50 a.m. On Tuesday, Governor Tina Kotek signed Senate Bill 1599 into law. The legislation moves the Gas Tax Referendum to the May Primary Election. State Senator Christine Drazan said the measure received no Republican support and virtually no backing outside of Supermajority Democratic politicians and their political allies. Senator Drazan released...

entry.title: DAVID BROCK SMITH LAUNCHES CAMPAIGN FOR U.S. SENATE
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 3:40 a.m. On Tuesday, Oregon State Senator David Brock Smith officially launched his campaign for the United State Senate seat currently held by Democrat Jeff Merkley. A campaign release said Brock Smith is a third-generation resident of southwest Oregon and former restaurateur of his family’s built and owned restaurant for 45 years....

entry.title: DESPAIN PRAISES BENTZ’S O&C RENEWAL ACT
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 3:30 a.m. This week, Republican 4th Congressional District candidate, retired Air Force Colonel Monque DeSpain expressed strong support for legislation introduced by Congressman Cliff Bentz to restore time production as the primary purpose of Oregon and California Railroad grant lands. A campaign release said the O&C Renewal Act of 2026, H.R. 7603,...

entry.title: WORK HAS RESUMED ON OREGON 42 LOOKINGGLASS CREEK TO I-5 PROJECT
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 3:20 a.m. A contractor working for the Oregon Department of Transportation has resumed work on the Oregon 42 Lookingglass Creek to I-5 Project. Dan Latham of ODOT said the contractor is installing reflective plastic bollards along the highway center median, near Rolling Hills Road and Landers Avenue. Latham said these are meant...

entry.title: DOUGLAS ELECTRIC COOPERATIVE SCHEDULES ANNUAL MEETING
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 3:05 a.m. The annual meeting of members of the Douglas Electric Cooperative will be held on Saturday, April 4th in the Community Conference Hall at the Douglas County Fairgrounds. Doors open at 9:00 a.m. for a social time with the business meeting beginning at 10:30 a.m. Lunch will be provided at 12:00...

entry.title: MISS DOUGLAS COUNTY CROWNS TITLEHOLDERS ON SUNDAY
entry.summary: March 4, 2026 2:50 a.m. On Sunday, the Miss Douglas County Scholarship program crowned its 2026 title holders. Program Executive Director Emily Warren Wylie said the organization awarded over $75,000 in scholarships to its 23 delegates competing in the event in the Jacoby Auditorium at Umpqua Community College. Warren Wylie said the amount increased due...

entry.title: Free Veteran Mental Health Documentary Screening Set For March 21 In Roseburg
entry.summary: A free public screening of the documentary “HERE. IS. BETTER.” will be held Saturday, March 21, 2026, at noon at the Roseburg Public Library. The event includes a panel discussion moderated by Returning Veterans Project Executive Director Amy Almond-Schmid. Admission is free.

entry.title: TRUE WEALTH 03.03.26
entry.summary: Presented by Littlejohn Financial, David Littlejohn and Matt Dickson have our weekly local financial program.

entry.title: MORNING CONVERSATION 03.03.26
entry.summary: Roseburg High School theater instructor Troy Pennington talks about their production of, “You’re a Good Man, Charlie Brown”, which continues later this week.

entry.title: Body Recovered, Man Identified After South Umpqua River Drowning
entry.summary: Body of a man who went under in the South Umpqua River near Portland Avenue on Feb. 27 was recovered March 2 by the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office Dive Team. He was identified as 46-year-old Johnathon Michael Laine of Roseburg. The case remains under investigation as a boating incident.

entry.title: MAN WHO DROWNED IN SOUTH UMPQUA RIVER RECOVERED AND IDENTIFIED
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:15 p.m. Divers from the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office Dive Team have recovered the body of the man who drowned in the South Umpqua River last Friday. A DCSO release said on Monday at approximately 8:00 a.m. deputies and Search and Rescue volunteers began conducting a follow-up search in the South Umpqua...

entry.title: Hydrant Flushing Scheduled Across Roseburg City Limits April 1 Through June 30
entry.summary: Roseburg will flush fire hydrants citywide April 1 to June 30, 2026, weekdays from 8 a.m. to 5 p.m. Temporary low pressure or discolored water may occur. Water is safe to drink, but run it until clear before washing clothes or dishes.

entry.title: Accidental Cigarette Disposal Blamed For Green Mobile Home Fire
entry.summary: Firefighters extinguished a structure fire in a single-wide home on Apricot Lane at Evergreen Village Mobile Home Park in Green within about 10 minutes of arrival. Central Douglas Fire & Rescue said the cause was accidental, likely improper cigarette disposal.

entry.title: STRUCTURE FIRE IN GREEN DISTRICT DETERMINED TO BE ACCIDENTAL
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 1:50 p.m. A structure fire in the Green District on Monday has been determined to be accidental. Central Douglas Fire & Rescue Fire Marshal Josh Waechter said shortly after 11:00 a.m. crews were dispatched to the fire on Apricot Lane in the Evergreen Village Mobile Home Park, just west of Interstate Five....

entry.title: INSIDE DOUGLAS COUNTY 03.03.26
entry.summary: Republican candidate for Governor, Christine Drazan talks about her campaign and why she believes she is the best candidate to replace incumbent Tina Kotek.

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR WARRANTS
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 11:00 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for warrants on Monday afternoon. An RPD report said just before 1:00 p.m. officers contacted the 38-year-old in the 200 block of Southeast Jackson Street. He was found to have two warrants for his arrest. The suspect admitted to having a user amount of fentanyl...

entry.title: FUGITIVE JAILED IN SOUTH COUNTY BY DEPUTIES ON MONDAY
entry.summary: March 8, 2026 10:40 a.m. A fugitive was arrested in south county by deputies with the Douglas County Sheriff’s Office on Monday. A DCSO report said at 8:15 a.m. a deputy allegedly observed the 30-year-old smoking methamphetamine under the Azalea-Glen Road Bridge in the Azalea area. The deputy confronted the man and told him to...

entry.title: TRANSIENT JAILED FOR WARRANTS AFTER BEING REPORTED AS MISSING
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 10:20 a.m. A Yoncalla area transient was jailed for warrants after being reported as missing on Monday. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said the 33-year-old Yoncalla area woman and a male were near the 2500 block of Yokum Road in the Riddle area. The woman wandered off toward a steep mountain...

entry.title: You, me and the bees
entry.summary: When I was a kid, I loved trips to Kruz Farms. It was where we went every year for a tractor ride to pick out our pumpkins. Sometimes it was for U-picking strawberries or to get produce from the stand.…

author.name: Kalee Paxton and Logan Bennett For The News-Review

entry.title: TRANSIENT JAILED FOR REPORTED BURGLARY INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 10:10 a.m. A transient was jailed following a reported burglary incident on Monday. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said at 6:00 p.m. a caller reported a vehicle was in her neighbor’s field in the 100 block of Preschern Lane, off of Old Melrose Road. An older model pickup was located buried...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL WOMAN FOR PROBATION VIOLATION
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 9:50 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a woman for a probation violation on Monday. An RPD report said at 9:15 a.m. officers responded to the 900 block of Northeast Rowe Street after a caller said there was a disturbance. The woman returned on probation and is not supposed to have contact with...

entry.title: ROSEBURG MAN JAILED FOLLOWING MOTOR VEHICLE/ELUDE INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 9:40 a.m. A Roseburg man was jailed following a motor vehicle and elude incident on Monday. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said at 11:15 a.m. male and female victims entered a store in the 4400 block of Old Highway 99 South in Green when they saw an unknown male, later identified...

entry.title: SMART Reading hosts fundraiser at Two Shy Brewing
entry.summary: ROSEBURG — SMART Reading will host a pint night fundraiser on 6-8 p.m. Tuesday at Two Shy Brewing, 1308 NW Park St. in Roseburg.

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN ON MENACING CHARGES FOLLOWING REPORTED ATTACK
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 9;30 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man on menacing charges after a reported attack on Monday morning. An RPD report said following an investigation where 32-year-old Perry Murphy had gone into a victim’s residence, allegedly threatened to kill the man and attacked him with a metal police, then pointed a firearm at...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL PAIR FOR ALLEGED TRESPASS INCIDENT
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 9:15 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed two people following an alleged trespass incident on Monday. An RPD report said at 10:15 a.m. officers contacted the 36-year-old woman and 44-year-old man inside a vacant building in the 2400 block of Northeast Diamond Lake Boulevard after getting information that they had been entering the building...

entry.title: Attempt To Elude Arrest Made After Stolen Vehicle Stop Attempt In Roseburg
entry.summary: Roseburg police arrested a 30-year-old man after a stolen vehicle linked to a robbery failed to yield during a stop attempt. Spike strips were deployed near NE Stephens and NE Garden Valley, and the vehicle stopped in the roadway. He was lodged at Corrections.

entry.title: Police Log: March 2, 2026
entry.summary: Roseburg Police Department made multiple arrests and citations, including a stolen-vehicle elude arrest, warrant and trespass arrests, a probation-violation detainer, and DUII arrests tied to suspected impairment and drug possession.

entry.title: Roseburg Arm Wrestling Club hosts Bronze Crown, first in Triple Crown Series
entry.summary: WINSTON — Boone Pearson said he began arm wrestling for fun when at bars, then he met people who showed him the technical side of the game and he got hooked.

entry.title: Roseburg Armwrestling Club hosts Bronze Crown, first in Triple Crown Series
entry.summary: WINSTON — Boone Pearson said he began arm wrestling for fun when at bars, then he met people who showed him the technical side of the game and he got hooked.

entry.summary: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop

entry.summary: March 3, 2026 4:00 a.m. A Winter Weather Advisory is in effect from 4:00 a.m. to 10:00 p.m. Wednesday above 5,000 feet, for mountain areas in parts of Southern Oregon. Forecasters at the National Weather Service in Medford said snow is expected with accumulations of 7 to 10 inches. Winds gusting as high as 40...

entry.title: SKARLATOS MAKES STATEMENT ON BOYCOTTING GAS TAX VOTE
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:50 a.m. On Monday, State Representative Alek Skarlatos issued a statement following his decision to walk out on a floor session before a vote was taken to move the gas tax referendum from the November ballot to the May Primary Election. Skarlatos said, “Today I left the floor before the vote on...

entry.title: TRACTOR SUPPLY COMPANY PLANS TO OPEN IN MARCH
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:50 a.m. In the KQEN Business Spotlight: Tractor Supply Company plans to open its new store in Roseburg later this month. A TSC spokesperson told News Radio 93-9 FM and 1240 KQEN that the soft opening is set for March 21st with the grand opening on March 28th. The store will be...

entry.title: 5TH ANNUAL MUSIC AT THE COURTHOUSE CONCERT ON WEDNESDAY
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:40 a.m. Douglas County Commissioners 5th annual Music at the Courthouse Concert will be this Wednesday, March 4th, featuring local schools and music programs. It is open to the public. A county release said music will be presented from 10:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. Bands and choirs from eight Douglas County schools...

entry.title: NEXTDOOR PLATFORMS SAYS HUCREST IS ROSEBURG’ FRIENDLIEST NEIGHBORHOOD
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:30 a.m. Nextdoor Holdings, which operates the Nextdoor Platform, has released its 2026 rankings of the friendliest Roseburg area neighborhoods. A Nextdoor release said the results spotlight the communities where neighbors are most engaged, supportive and welcoming. Data captures everything from neighbors helping each other out to how they share resources and...

entry.title: GREATEST OF THE GRAPE HAPPENING THIS SATURDAY NIGHT
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:20 a.m. In the KQEN Cultural Spotlight: Oregon’s oldest wine celebration is happening this Saturday. The Greatest of the Grape is presented by the Umpqua Valley Winegrowers Association. It will take place Saturday night from 6:00 p.m. to 9:0 pm. at Seven Feathers Casino Resort in Canyonville. The 55th annual event features...

entry.summary: March 3, 2026 3:05 a.m. In the KQEN Cultural Spotlight: Umpqua Community College Performing and Visual Arts presents the Roseburg Concert Chorale and Umpqua Chamber Orchestra in concert on Friday, March 13th at 7:00 p.m. and Sunday, March 15th at 3:00 p.m. in the Whipple Fine Arts Center on the UCC campus. The concert will...

entry.title: ELKTON MAN JAILED AFTER BEING FOUND INSIDE A SHOP
entry.summary: March 3, 2026 2:50 a.m. An Elkton man was jailed after being found inside a shop on Sunday. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said just after 12:00 p.m. deputies were advised that the 43-year-old man was at the property in the 800 block of Azalea Drive in Elkton. Units arrived and located the suspect...

entry.title: Autism Awareness Walk Draws Crowd in Roseburg Amid Fremont Middle School Case
entry.summary: Roseburg community members gathered Sunday for an Autism Awareness Solidarity Walk for Justice, marching from Fir Grove Park to Fremont Middle School after the arrests of three school employees tied to the Developmental Learning Center.

entry.title: INSIDE DOUGLAS COUNTY 03.02.26
entry.summary: Live from Salem, State Representative Court Boice with an update on legislation as the short session is in its final days.

entry.title: Man Arrested After Reported Stick Assault At Stewart Park
entry.summary: Man arrested after police said a man sleeping under the Stewart Park pavilion was struck in the head with a large stick. Officers reported the victim suffered a laceration and was transported to Mercy Medical Center. The suspect fled, was tased and lodged at corrections.

entry.title: Man presumed drowned after South Umpqua River rescue attempt
entry.summary: ROSEBURG, Ore. - A man is presumed drowned after an emergency incident in the South Umpqua River on Friday.

entry.title: Police Log: February 27 - March 2, 2026
entry.summary: Roseburg police reported arrests and citations Feb. 27–Mar. 2, including multiple DUII arrests, a Stewart Park assault, theft and trespass cases, warrants, and probation/parole violations, according to City of Roseburg Police Logs.

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE CHARGE MAN WITH HARASSMENT
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 11:00 a.m. Roseburg Police charged a man with harassment on Sunday afternoon. An RPD report said at 2:40 p.m. officers responded to the 1000 block of Southeast Main Street after a caller said there had been a disturbance. During the investigation, it was determined that the 68-year-old had bit a victim on...

entry.title: Man Presumed Drowned After Being Swept Downstream In South Umpqua River
entry.summary: Man presumed drowned after a reported river rescue Friday morning on the South Umpqua River near Portland Avenue. Call came in about 7:23 a.m. Deputies and partner agencies searched for hours. Additional search efforts will continue.

It was confirmed and the man was taken into...

entry.summary: March 2, 2026 10:30 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a parole violation and unlawful possession of methamphetamine on Friday. An RPD report said at 12:00 p.m. officers contacted the 50-year-old in the 200 block of Northeast Jackson Street after observing him with an open beer. Officers knew that as a stipulation of his...

entry.title: WOMAN TAKEN TO HOSPITAL AFTER SINGLE-VEHICLE CRASH
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 10:20 a.m. A woman was taken to the hospital after a single vehicle crash on Sunday night. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said at approximately 11:10 p.m. a driver and a passenger were headed downhill on Longs Ranch near the 7000 block of Melqua Road. There was thick fog in the...

entry.title: MAN PRESUMED DROWNED IN THE SOUTH UMPQUA RIVER
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 10:10 a.m. A man is presumed drowned following an incident in the South Umpqua River on Friday. A Douglas County Sheriff’s Office report said at approximately 7:20 a.m. 911 dispatchers received a report of a man stranded in the middle of the river, yelling for help. DCSO along with multiple other agencies...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR THEFT INCIDENT AND WARRANT
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 10:00 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a theft incident and a warrant on Friday. An RPD report said at 10:20 a.m. officers responded to the 2100 block of Northeast Diamond Lake Boulevard after the 42-year-old allegedly grabbed an employee before leaving the business. Officers contacted the suspect a short distance...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE ADD MORE SEX CRIME CHARGES TO MAN ALREADY IN JAIL
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 9:40 a.m. Roseburg Police added more sex crime charges on Friday, to a man that was already in jail. An RPD report said additional review of digital evidence related to the original case allegedly revealed multiple new sex crimes involving the same victim. As a result, at 11:30 a.m. 41-year-old Gregory Van...

entry.title: ROSEBURG POLICE JAIL MAN FOR REPORTED ASSAULT AT STEWART PARK
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 9:20 a.m. Roseburg Police jailed a man for a reported assault in Stewart Park early Friday morning. An RPD report said shortly after 5:30 a.m. officers contacted a victim who said he had been assaulted by an unknown man at the park, in the 1000 block of Northwest Stewart Parkway. The victim...

entry.title: MORNING CONVERSATION 3.2.26
entry.summary: Umpqua Valley Winegrowers Executive Director Ali Rodgers talks about their Greatest of the Grape event, happening on Saturday at Seven Feathers Casino and Resort.

entry.title: Miss Douglas County Scholarship program crowns new titleholders
entry.summary: WINCHESTER — Rebecca Ngigi said she walked into the Miss Douglas County Scholarship program expecting to wear pretty dresses and dedicate her time to serving the community and as of Sunday she is even more involved.

entry.title: March 2, 2026
entry.summary: The Roseburg Receiver Morning Scoop

entry.title: SENATORS RESPOND TO U.S. MILITARY ACTION IN IRAN
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 6:10 a.m. On Saturday, both of Oregon’s U.S. Senators responded to U.S. military action in Iran. Senator Ron Wyden said, “Donald Trump is unilaterally dragging us into another costly and deadly war in the Middle East that the American people don’t want”. Wyden said, “Trump and Hegseth are putting American lives on...

entry.title: ROSEBURG RECEIVES ABOVE NORMAL RAIN FOR FEBRUARY
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 3:50 a.m. The Umpqua Valley received an above normal amount of rain for February but is still significantly behind the amount of precipitation usually received for this point in the rain year. Forecasters at the National Weather Service in Medford told News Radio 93-9 FM and 1240 KQEN that the Roseburg Regional...

entry.title: MYRTLE CREEK CITY COUNCIL MEETING MONDAY NIGHT
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 3:40 a.m. The Myrtle Creek City Council has a meeting on Monday night at 5:30 p.m. The agenda includes the consideration of approving a three-year intergovernmental agreement between the city and the Myrtle Creek Rural Fire Protection District to begin in the upcoming fiscal year. The council will look at the future...

entry.title: WINSTON CITY COUNCIL MEETING MONDAY NIGHT
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 3:30 a.m. The Winston City Council will meet on Monday night. Councilors will consider a resolution to authorize the city manager to enter into an intergovernmental agreement with Douglas County to continue providing law enforcement dispatch services for the upcoming fiscal year. The new contract is increasing by three percent for a...

entry.title: EARLY LITERACY EVENT HAPPENING MARCH 9TH
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 3:30 a.m. Roseburg Schools will host their third and final early childhood literacy event of the season, next Monday, March 9th, as part of the district’s grant-funded efforts intended to enhance literacy development across the community. A Roseburg Schools release said all Roseburg-area families with children ages 2 to 7 are invited...

entry.title: SPRING INTO GARDENING RETURNS ON SATURDAY
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 3:05 a.m. The Spring into Gardening Conference is returning this Saturday to Umpqua Community College. Hosted by the Oregon State University Extension Douglas County Small Farms Program and Douglas County Master Gardener Association, this one-day horticulture event offers a full schedule of educational sessions designed to help participants cultivate healthier, more resilient...

entry.title: FREE VETERAN MENTAL HEALTH DOCUMENTARY SCREENING COMING
entry.summary: March 2, 2026 2:50 a.m. A free public screening of the documentary “HERE.IS.BETTER”. will take place on Saturday, March 21st, starting at 12:00 p.m. at the Roseburg Public Library on Northeast Diamond Lake Boulevard. The event is presented by the Returning Veterans Project, with local support from The Cow Creek Band of Umpqua Tribe of...

entry.title: Roseburg parents rally after Fremont school staff arrests
entry.summary: Three Fremont Middle School instructors in Roseburg have been arrested and charged after an investigation into student injuries in the Developmental Learning Center, prompting community outrage and parent-led advocacy events for justice and awareness.
